subject: Physics
title: The Big Bang: How It All Started
description: One-minute skill: The Big Bang: How It All Started.

hook.text: 13.8 billion years ago, the entire universe was smaller than an atom. Then it exploded into existence.

buildup.text: There was no space before the Big Bang. Space itself was created in the expansion.

discovery.text: In the first second, quarks formed protons and neutrons. In three minutes, hydrogen and helium nuclei appeared.

twist.text: The Big Bang wasn't an explosion in space—it was an explosion of space. Every point in the universe was the center.

climax.text: For 380,000 years, the universe was an opaque plasma. Then it cooled enough for light to travel freely.

punchline.text: No center, no edge. Space itself expanding from a point smaller than an atom.

quiz.question: What happened during the Big Bang?
options: Space itself expanded from an extremely dense, hot state
A star exploded and scattered debris
Two galaxies collided and created matter
